title: Air Traffic Controller

rationales.taskAutomation: AI conflict-detection and decision-support tools assist controllers, but final separation and clearance authority is a licensed human responsibility with zero tolerance for error.
rationales.toolMaturity: Automation aids (NextGen tools, conflict alerts) are shipped and used, but full autonomous air-traffic management is not a deployed or certified product.
rationales.adoption: FAA facilities use automation aids widely, but as decision support only; staffing has not been reduced by AI to date.
rationales.agenticExposure: The role's safety-critical, split-second liability keeps it outside where autonomous agents currently operate.

tasksAtRisk: Routine traffic-flow monitoring in low-density airspace
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-2021, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI decision-support tool oversight and override judgment

insight: Controllers already work alongside mature automation aids, but the FAA has not reduced staffing because final separation authority stays with a certified human; near-term risk is low, concentrated instead in tool-assisted efficiency gains rather than headcount reduction.
